# Page Title: Chemical Engineering | IIT Jammu

Chemical Engineering is one of the “BIG FOUR” disciplines of the
Engineering Science, the rest being Civil, Mechanical, and Electrical. It is also
the youngest of the basic branches of the engineering discipline. A chemical
engineer works in a place like the one seen in the image above. They are responsible
for converting any raw material to a finished product, may it be petrol from crude
oil, synthetic rubber from rubber sap, tea bags from tea leaves, sugar from
sugarcane, the list goes on.

Having a humble origin in 1888, under the vision of Gorge E Davis, Chemical
Engineering has come a long way over the century. The chemical engineers, have
provided solutions to global food problems, the environmental pollution problems,
energy needs (in terms of gas and gasoline), the longevity of human health and so
on. It is a dynamic discipline whose products reach and affect all life. Right from
when you wake up to you go to bed, chemical engineering is always there with you,
making your life that a bit more comfortable.

Over the last century the discipline has changed a lot of focus. Chemical
Engineering, has moved from its traditional forte of Oil and Gas now to
Pharmaceuticals to Biological production. All this is possible because a chemical
engineer is trained in physics, chemistry, mathematics and economics including
biological and environmental sciences. They are now being called for to make life
simple and solve the challenges that haunt the country today, may it be the issue of
air pollution near the capital area, valorization of solid waste generated in major
metropolitan, solving the water pollution and provide safe drinking water, better
power generation devices, better medical devices and infrastructure and so on. I
would urge you to have a look at the following video that has a brief idea of what
chemical engineers do.

A chemical engineering graduate has ample of opportunities to work with many national
and international companies.
All over the world there is always a shortage of good chemical engineers. To say,
three of the top ten Fortune 500
companies are chemical companies and chemical companies always make into this list
under every odd. A graduate can
choose from avid number of areas in chemical engineering to build their careers, may
it be petroleum or
petrochemical, polymers, water and wastewater, fine/bulk chemicals, pharmaceuticals,
natural products, pulp and
paper, food and beverages, nuclear energy, biomedical devices, paints and dyes, the
list just goes on. A chemical
engineer grows higher financially as they gain experience, in majority of the cases
the growth is exponential.

# Page Title: Chemical Engineering | IIT Jammu

The Department of Chemical Engineering, IIT Jammu, celebrated its Foundation Day on 17th
August 2024. The event featured insightful talks from distinguished guests across academia
and industry. Prof. Nishit Verma discussed transitioning towards a sustainable future by
going beyond conventional materials and methods. Sh. J.N. Sharma highlighted the role of
public administration in environmental governance, while Sh. Siddharth Shankar spoke on the
green technologies developed at Lummus Technology. Sh. Ravi Kumar Voolapalli shared valuable
insights on refining processes and sustainable development.

The event concluded with the awarding of prizes and certificates to the winners and
participants of the Idea Pitch and Poster Presentation. A heartfelt note of thanks was
expressed by Dr. Ravi Kumar Arun, the Head of the Department.

## From The Desk of HoD

Welcome to the Department of Chemical Engineering (ChE) at IIT Jammu.
The Department of Chemical Engineering was established in 2018 with the
intake of the first undergraduate batch. Since then the department has
grown to the first batch graduating in 2021. The department is currently
growing and consists of nine faculty members: assistant professors,
visiting professors, and professors. With a unique combination of
enthusiasm, experience, and adaptability, the department has expanded
from modest beginnings to astounding new heights.

Dr. Ravi Kumar Arun
Head of Department

#### Amrita Chatterjee

Dr. Amrita Chatterjee is currently employed as an Assistant Professor in Chemical Engineering department at IIT Jammu. She is working on green energy technologies, with research spanning over batteries, supercapacitors, photo/electrocatalysis, and biomass valorization. Her work focuses on designing functional porous materials like MOF, COF, and hierarchical carbon and engineering these next generation materials to device-level solutions for sustainable energy and chemical conversion.

Kindly email at amrita.chatterjee@iitjammu.ac.in for any possible collaborations or interest.

##### Research Interest

1) Pore engineering in metal and covalent organic frameworks ( MOFs and COFs) and hierarchical carbon.

2) Electrode design and synthesis for lithium ion and sodium ion batteries and supercapacitors.

3) Electrolyte synthesis and interface engineering in solid state batteries for room temperature and sub zero applications.

4) Photo and electrocatalysis for waste- to- energy via CO2 reduction, water splitting, organic transformation, quantifying intrinsic kinetics versus mass transport.

##### Education Qualification

1) PhD, Chemical Engineering, Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, Hong Kong, 2018.

2) B.Tech, Chemical Engineering, National Institute of Technology, Durgapur, India, 2011

##### Teaching Engagements

Reaction engineering, Mass transfer, Heat transfer, Sustainability and LCA , Process safety

#### Dnyaneshwar Ramloo Bhawangirkar

**Dr. Dnyaneshwar Bhawangirkar** is presently serving as an Assistant Professor in the department of chemical engineering at IIT Jammu in India.

His research interest is in ‘Gas Hydrates or Clathrate Hydrates’ focusing on various applications, including methane recovery from the natural gas hydrates present beneath the seafloor, carbon sequestration in geological marine sedimentary locations, hydrogen and natural gas storage, and flow assurance through both modelling and experimental approaches. He is also exploring different strategies for improving the oil recovery via novel EOR technologies.

Kindly email at **dnyaneshwar.bhawangirkar@iitjammu.ac.in** for any possible collaborations.

##### Research Interest

Gas hydrates or clathrate hydrates for their applications in NGH development, CCS, energy storage and flow assurance (Modelling and Experiments)

1. Methane recovery from natural gas hydrates (NGH development)
2. Carbon capture and sequestration in porous medium as hydrates (CCS)
3. Natural gas and hydrogen storage as hydrates (Energy storage)
4. Hydrate inhibition in oil and gas pipelines for flow assurance

Additionally, we are also equally interested in developing strategies for enhancing the oil recovery (EOR) through different approaches.

##### Education Qualification

Carbon capture storage and utilization (CCSU), Process Intensification, Environmental Engineering, Biochemical and Bioprocess Engineering, Waste to wealth, Nanomaterial synthesis and characterization, Photochemistry and Photocatalysis, Plasmonics, X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, Heterogeneous catalysis (tri-phasic reactions), Nanofluids, Process design, Teaching and Learning in Higher Education (TLHE).

## Dr. Dharitri Rath

#### Message

Welcome to the Department of Chemical Engineering (ChE) at IIT Jammu. The Department of Chemical Engineering was established in 2018 with the intake of the first undergraduate batch. Since then the department has grown to the first batch graduating in 2021. The department is currently growing and consists of nine faculty members: assistant professors, visiting professors, and professors. With a unique combination of enthusiasm, experience, and adaptability, the department has expanded from modest beginnings to astounding new heights.

The vision of the department is “To be an epitome of excellence in chemical engineering science with a world-class multidisciplinary approach towards sustained growth in academics and research with impetus for service to society”. The department is working to reach new heights by bringing together faculty from different fields in order to accomplish this.

The department currently offers 4 year undergraduate, 2 year M.Tech program with specialization in Sustainable Energy Engineering and Ph.D. program in Chemical Engineering. The also contributes in Minor in Nuclear Engineering, Minor in Bioscience and Bioengineering as well as Engineering and Scientific Computation.

The department has identified different thrust areas of research ranging from Energy and Environment to Biomedical Devices, Biochemical Engineering to Catalysis and Reactor Engineering, Modelling and Simulation to Drug Design and Delivery. Having a high level of diversity in research the department has many high-end analytical and experimental facilities. The department asserts student towards interdisciplinary research that would lead new avenues of entrepreneurship that lies in between. Identification of the gaps in the market and provide sustainable solutions to those problems by students is highly encouraged.

Currently the department has identified a few thrust areas keeping in mind regional and national interests. Chemical Engineers are currently addressing the issues of Sustainable Chemical Processing, Process Intensification, Agro-chemical processing and Carbon Free Energy Systems at multiple scales (macro, conventional and micro-nano). They explore the mega possibilities at the intersection of traditional engineering and new technologies (genomics, microfluidics, nano) using Big Data Analytics and AI Predictive Models. To take part in the 21st Century “new agro” based revolution they can exploit the rich biodiversity of Union Territories of J&K Ladakh. Entrepreneurship and enterprise are one of the major ethos of the department. These skills and this approach will help the budding Chemical Engineer to achieve path breaking careers in emerging markets.

# Page Title: Chemical Engineering | IIT Jammu

The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu), in partnership with the Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC), Department of Atomic Energy, Government of India, organized a one-day Scientific and Technical Outreach Program on the 13th of October, 2023. The event took place at the scenic IIT Jammu campus, nestled in the serene surroundings of Nagrota, Jammu.

This engaging program saw active participation from numerous schools and local colleges in Jammu, including Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya Gharota and Government College for Women Gandhinagar, with over 120 enthusiastic students in attendance. The inaugural ceremony was graced by the esteemed presence of Prof Manoj Singh Gaur, Director at IIT Jammu. Accompanying him were distinguished scientists from BARC, including Dr Archana Sharma, Director of Beam Technology Development; Mr S. Prabhakar, specializing in Food Irradiation Technology; Mr Sameer Mandal, expert in Nuclear Reactor technology; Mr R. K. B. Yadav, specialist in Radiation safety in health & environment; Mrs Herman Rao, Mr R. K. Mishra, and Mr Yatin Thakur, too, were present.

The IIT Jammu team was equally well-represented by Prof Kannan Iyer (Dean of Academics), Dr Vijayan Pallipattu (Dean of Administration), and Prof Debapriya Mondal. Prof Yogesh Nimdeo, the Head of the Chemical Engineering Department, also actively participated in this enlightening program. Furthermore, other institutions contributed to the success of the event, with Dr B. D. Sehgal (GWC) and Mr Awadhesh Kumar (JNVG) and their colleagues actively engaged with the attendees.

The Scientific and Technical Outreach Program, organized by the Public Awareness Section of the Scientific Information Resource Division at BARC, extended over a week, from the 9th to the 13th of October, 2023, across five colleges in Jammu. It culminated in a final and remarkable session at IIT Jammu. The overarching objectives of the program were threefold:

- To dispel myths and misconceptions about atomic energy and radiation.
- To acquaint students and the general public with the functioning of reactors and BARC technological innovations, with a focus on Food irradiation.
- To enlighten students about the potential career opportunities in the nuclear sector and the groundbreaking innovations within it.

Eminent scientists conducted extensive discussions and interactions with students and faculty, complemented by two captivating skits on nuclear energy and BARC Technologies.

The program saw the active involvement of numerous specialists from various BARC departments, including all the aforementioned scientists, as well as technical staff members such as Mr Ram Badade, Mr Atul Likhite, Mr Umesh Gulhane, Mr Pradeep Pawar, and Mrs Samruddhi Patil. Their informative lectures and engaging skits left an indelible impact on the participants.

The response from the students who attended the workshop and participated in the quiz sessions was overwhelmingly positive and encouraging. Their enthusiasm has generated numerous requests for more such programs, reaffirming the success of this outreach initiative.

In an effort to bridge the gap between academia and industry, the Department of Chemical
Engineering, IIT Jammu organised an industrial visit for UG and PG students to Kuantum
Papers Ltd.

The industrial visit to Kuantum offered an insight into production techniques, safety
protocols, quality control, and the role of innovation in enhancing productivity. The
students visited their in-house nursery which ensures continuous supply with a variety of
quality raw materials for sustainable development. Students were also briefed about
different steps involved in the process of transformation of pulp form agro-residues,
hardwood, veneer chips, and bamboo as raw materials. Interaction of students with Engineers
and DCS Operators form Pulp Mill and Solvent recovery section helped our students to relate
theory with large manufacturing plant operations.

The visit also provided an opportunity for students to Interacting with professionals and
gaining insights from industry experts. The visit concluded with a vote of thanks from HoD
Chemical Engineering Dr. Ravi Kumar Arun and Associate Vice President HR (KPL) - Sh. Ajay
Sharma

**Celebrating the Success of the Workshop: "Micro-fabrication and Biosensors:
Advances in
Diagnostics"**

We are delighted to share the successful conclusion of our two-day workshop,
**"Micro-fabrication and
Biosensors: Advances in Diagnostics,"** held from January 18th to 19th,
2025, at the Department of
Chemical Engineering, IIT Jammu. The event brought together an exceptional panel of speakers
from renowned
institutions, including **IIT-KGP, IIT BHU, GMC Jammu, AIIMS Jammu, Max Healthcare,
CSIR-IIIM, CSIR-CMERI,
CSIR-NPL, MNNIT Allahabad, and KIET Delhi.**

Our esteemed speakers captivated the audience with insightful sessions on cutting-edge topics
like **MEMS
devices, clinical biosensors, AI in healthcare, innovative materials, and advanced
diagnostics.**

We extend our heartfelt gratitude to our Convener, **Dr. Ravi Kumar Arun**, and
Co-convenor, **Dr.
Vinay Sharma**, for their visionary leadership. A special thanks to our
sponsors—**@JKSTIC,
@BharatScientific, @BiotechnicalResource,@Batsolequipments, and
@IIT-JAMMU**—for their generous
support, which made this event possible.

Participants had the unique opportunity to engage in hands-on lab sessions and explore
the**Paloura campus,
Receptor Biology Lab, Microfluidic Design and Bioengineering Lab, Advanced Manufacturing
Lab, Thermal and
Natural Phenomenon Lab, Microfluidics and Energy Systems Lab**, and the
**CIF facility** of
IIT Jammu. These experiences provided invaluable practical insights into biosensor
integration and advanced
diagnostic technologies. We sincerely thank everyone who contributed to the success of this
workshop. Together, we
are fostering innovation, collaboration, and excellence in the field of diagnostics.

Stay connected for more exciting initiatives and opportunities!
